title: stenophragma virgatum
intro: Stenophragma virgatum, also known as the "sensitive plant," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is a perennial herb native to South America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throats. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Stenophragma virgatum can be used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
